articles.title: The Reinvention of Workday: From System of Record to Platform of Agents
summary: Workday is repositioning its HRIS from a transactional system of record into a platform that hosts AI “transformation agents” across HR and finance workflows.
Its acquisitions of Paradox and Sana signal a bet on a few large, capable agents rather than hundreds of narrow mini-agents.
Implication for HR leaders: data quality and platform governance become the deciding factors in whether agentic HRIS delivers value.

articles.title: The State of AI in HR 2026 Report
summary: 57% of organisations still run core HR systems with no AI functionality, exposing a wide gap between ambition and current HRIS capability.
Only about 1 in 4 HR professionals played a leading role in their organisation's AI implementation.
Calls for joint HR–IT governance frameworks, shared data standards, and a clear plan for privacy, security and the skills gap.

articles.title: Federal court to consider AI in hiring as Workday bias case advances
summary: The Mobley v. Workday case advances after a judge declined to dismiss claims, putting AI-driven hiring tools under direct legal scrutiny.
Plaintiffs argue Workday's recommendation engine disadvantaged applicants over 40, raising disparate-impact questions for AI screening.
Takeaway: HR now owns AI bias and trust—deployed tools need to be explainable, auditable and defensible.

articles.title: The Great Reinvention of Human Resources Has Begun
summary: Argues that AI is forcing a structural rethink of the HR operating model, not just an automation of existing tasks.
HR shifts from process administrator to designer and governor of AI-augmented people systems.
Skills, data architecture and change management are framed as the real constraints on transformation.

articles.title: 3 charts on what CHROs think about AI and the future of their departments
summary: Most HR leaders report low confidence in their organisation's current AI capabilities.
CHROs expect AI to reshape department structure and roles, but cite skills and data readiness as blockers.
Reinforces that AI strategy in HR is still maturing—adoption is uneven across functions.

articles.title: AI in HR: A Comprehensive Guide [2026 Edition]
summary: A practical map of where AI lands across the employee lifecycle: sourcing, screening, onboarding, performance and HR support.
Over 80% of HR departments now use generative AI, typically as a “capable junior colleague” under human judgement.
Useful framing for leaders scoping which HR tasks to automate first and where to keep a human in the loop.
